title: IELTS General Training Mock Test 1
description: Complete IELTS General Training test with all 4 skills
instructions: Read the instructions for each part of the paper carefully. Answer all the questions. Write your answers in the answer sheet. You must complete the answer sheet within the time limit. At the end of the test, hand in both this question paper and your answer sheet.

words.example: The Earth formed approximately 4.5 billion years ago.

words.example: The Solar System consists of eight planets.
The Earth's surface is 71% water.
The idea of months originated from the moon.
The biosphere includes all habitats on Earth.

words.definition: To change from one form to another
words.example: Cyanobacteria convert sunlight to oxygen.
The Earth has a diameter of about 12,742 km.
The Earth rotates on its axis once a day.
Advantage Sales & Marketing offers extensive services.
Chipotle offers a flexible work schedule.
The Vitamin Shoppe has progressive policies.
PSA Healthcare provides compassionate care.

words.definition: A person being considered for a position
words.example: Candidates must have experience.
There are room for advancement opportunities.
PSA Healthcare provides comprehensive services.

You must evacuate during a fire.
Fire is a major hazard.
People must respond quickly to fire alarms.

words.definition: A measure taken to prevent something
words.example: Fire drills are an important precaution.

passages.title: The Earth

vocabList.example: The Earth formed approximately 4.5 billion years ago.

vocabList.definition: To be composed or made up of
vocabList.example: The Solar System consists of eight planets.
The idea of months originated from the moon.
The biosphere includes all habitats on Earth.

vocabList.definition: To change from one form to another

vocabList.example: The Earth has a diameter of about 12,742 km.
The Earth rotates on its axis once a day.

content: (A) The Earth is the third planet from the Sun and it is the only planet known to have life on it. The Earth formed around 4.5 billion years ago. It is one of four rocky planets on the inside of the Solar System. The other three are Mercury, Venus, and Mars.

(B) The large mass of the Sun makes the Earth move around it, just as the mass of the Earth makes the Moon move around it. The Earth also turns round in space, so different parts face the Sun at different times. The Earth goes around the Sun once (one 'year') for every 365¼ times it turns all the way around (one 'day').

(C) The Moon goes around the Earth about every 27⅓ days, and reflects light from the Sun. As the Earth goes round the Sun at the same time, the changing light of the Moon takes about 29½ days to go from dark to bright to dark again. That is where the idea of 'month' came from. However, now most months have 30 or 31 days so they fit into one year.

(D) The Earth is the only planet in our Solar System that has a large amount of liquid water. About 71% of the surface of the Earth is covered by oceans. Because of this, it is sometimes called the 'Blue Planet'.

(E) Because of its water, the Earth is home to millions of species of plants and animals. The things that live on Earth have changed its surface greatly. For example, early cyanobacteria changed the air and gave it oxygen. The living part of the Earth's surface is called the 'biosphere'.

(F) The Earth is part of the eight planets and many thousands of small bodies that move around the Sun as its Solar System. The Solar System is moving through the Orion Arm of the Milky Way Galaxy now, and will be for about the next 10,000 years.

(G) The Earth is generally 150,000,000 kilometers or 93,000,000 miles away from the Sun (this distance is named an 'Astronomical Unit'). The Earth moves along its way at an average speed of about 30 km or 19 mi a second. The Earth turns all the way around about 365¼ times in the time it takes for the Earth to go all the way around the Sun. To make up this extra bit of a day every year, an additional day is used every four years. This is named a 'leap year'.

(H) The Moon goes around the Earth at an average distance of 400,000 kilometers (250,000 mi). It is locked to Earth, so that it always has the same half facing the Earth; the other half is called the 'dark side of the Moon'. It takes about 27⅓ days for the Moon to go all the way around the Earth but, because the Earth is moving around the Sun at the same time, it takes about 29½ days for the Moon to go from dark to bright to dark again. This is where the word 'month' came from, even though most months now have 30 or 31 days.

subTasks.instruction: Which paragraph contains the following information? (1-8)

items.text: How long it takes the Earth to go around the Sun
How the Moon affects the calendar
The reason Earth is called the Blue Planet
How living things changed the Earth
The distance from the Earth to the Sun
Why the Moon always shows the same side to Earth

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C, or D.

items.text: According to the passage, what percentage of Earth's surface is water?

options.text: The part of Earth where life exists

passages.title: Company Profiles

vocabList.example: Advantage Sales & Marketing offers extensive services.
Chipotle offers a flexible work schedule.
The Vitamin Shoppe has progressive policies.

content: Advantage Sales & Marketing (ASM) is one of the country's leading sales and marketing companies. We offer extensive services in the retail, consumer goods, and healthcare industries. Our team of professionals helps clients increase their market share and build brand awareness.

Chipotle Mexican Grill is known for its commitment to fresh, high-quality ingredients. We offer a flexible work schedule and opportunities for advancement. Our culture focuses on attracting passionate individuals who share our love for good food.

The Vitamin Shoppe is a retailer of nutritional supplements. We offer progressive wellness programs for our employees and customers. Our mission is to help people live healthier lives through education and quality products.

PSA Healthcare provides home healthcare services. We are known for our compassionate care and professional staff. We offer competitive benefits and opportunities for career growth in the healthcare field.

subTasks.instruction: Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS for each answer.

items.text: What industry does Advantage Sales & Marketing primarily serve?
What does The Vitamin Shoppe sell?

passages.title: Fire Safety

vocabList.example: Fire drills prepare you for emergencies.
You must evacuate during a fire.
People must respond quickly to fire alarms.

vocabList.definition: A measure taken to prevent something
vocabList.example: Fire drills are an important precaution.

content: Fire safety is a critical aspect of building management and personal safety. Understanding how to respond in case of fire can save lives.
The first precaution is prevention. This includes regular inspection of electrical equipment, keeping flammable materials away from heat sources, and ensuring buildings meet fire safety codes.
Smoke detectors are essential safety devices. They provide early warning in case of fire, giving people time to evacuate. It is important to test smoke detectors monthly and replace batteries yearly.

Fire extinguishers should be readily accessible on each floor. Everyone should know how to use a fire extinguisher using the PASS method: Pull the pin, Aim at the base of the fire, Squeeze the handle, and Sweep from side to side.

In case of fire, remaining calm is crucial. Follow the established evacuation plan, stay low to avoid smoke, and use stairs not elevators. Once outside, go to the designated assembly point and call emergency services.

subTasks.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? Write TRUE, FALSE, or NOT GIVEN.

items.text: Fire prevention is the first step in fire safety.
Smoke detectors should be tested every month.
You should use elevators during a fire evacuation.

sections.title: Task 1: Letter
sections.instruction: You should spend about 20 minutes on this task. Write at least 150 words.
sections.prompt: You recently moved to a new city. Write a letter to a friend telling them about your new home and inviting them to visit.
bullets: Explain what you like about the area

sections.title: Task 2: Essay
sections.instruction: You should spend about 40 minutes on this task. Write at least 250 words.
sections.prompt: Some people believe that unpaid community service should be a compulsory part of high school programs. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
bullets: Include relevant examples from your knowledge or experience

listening.title: Listening
listening.instructions: You will hear a number of different recordings and you will have to answer questions on what you hear. There will be time for you to read the instructions and questions, and you will have a chance to check your work. All the recordings will be played once only. The test is in 4 parts. At the end of the test, you will be given 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the answer sheet.

sections.subtitle: Booking a Holiday Cottage
sections.description: You will hear a conversation between a travel agent and a customer who wants to book a holiday cottage. First, you have some time to look at questions 1-10.
sections.context: Conversation between two speakers - social situation

questions.instruction: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.text: Customer name:
Contact telephone:
Preferred location:
Number of adults:
Number of children:

questions.instruction: Answer the questions below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.text: What is the maximum budget per week?
Which facility must the cottage have?
What date does the customer want to arrive?
What is the customer's main concern about the cottage?
What does the customer ask to be sent?

sections.subtitle: Tour of a Museum Complex
sections.description: You will hear a guide giving a tour of a museum complex. First, you have some time to look at questions 11-20.

questions.instruction: Label the map below. Write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 11-15.

The museum's collection focuses mainly on

sections.subtitle: Discussion about University Course
sections.description: You will hear a discussion between a student and an academic advisor. First, you have some time to look at questions 21-30.
sections.context: Conversation between two speakers - academic context

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
questions.question: The student is studying
What is the main concern about the assignment?

sections.subtitle: Lecture on Animal Behavior
sections.description: You will hear a lecture about animal behavior. First, you have some time to look at questions 31-40.

questions.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.text: The study focuses on animal behavior in
The main finding is that animals

parts.title: Part 1: Introduction and Interview
parts.instruction: Answer the examiner's questions about familiar topics. Speak naturally and give full answers.

questions.text: What kind of place is it?
What's the most interesting part of your town/village?
What kind of jobs do the people in your town/village do?
Would you say it's a good place to live? (Why?)
Tell me about the kind of accommodation you live in.
How long have you lived there?
What do you like about living there?
What sort of accommodation would you most like to live in?

parts.title: Part 2: Long Turn
parts.instruction: You will have 1 minute to prepare, then speak for 1-2 minutes on the topic.

topicCard.topic: Describe something valuable that you own.
bulletPoints: explain why it is valuable to you

roundingQuestions.text: Is it valuable in terms of money?
Would it be easy to replace?

parts.title: Part 3: Discussion
parts.instruction: Discuss more abstract topics related to Part 2. Give reasons and examples.

questions.text: In general, what do you consider to be valuable possessions?
Do you think people place too much importance on material possessions?
How do advertisements influence what people consider valuable?
Do you think the things people value change as they get older?
